questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The colour of the $\mathrm{X}_2$ molecules of group 17 elements changes gradually from yellow to violet down the group. This is due to
options: The physical state of $\mathrm{X}_2$ at room temperature changes from gas to solid down the group
Decrease in HOMO-LUMO gap down the group
Decrease in $\pi^*-\sigma^*$ gap down the group
Decrease in ionization energy down the group
questions.solution:
Answer (B, C)
The colours arise from the absorption of light on promoting an electron from the ground state to higher state. On descending the group, the energy levels become closer and gap between HOMO - LUMO decreases.

HOMO is $\pi^*$
LUMO is $\sigma^*$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The nitrogen containing compound produced in the reaction of $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ with $\mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}$
options: Can also be prepared by reaction of $\mathrm{P}_4$ and $\mathrm{HNO}_3$
Reacts with Na metal producing a brown gas
questions.solution:
Answer (B, D)
$\mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}$ is dehydrating agent and dehydrate $\mathrm{HNO}_3$.
$2 \mathrm{HNO}_3 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{P}_4 \mathrm{O}_{10}} \mathrm{~N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is dia magnetic
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{NO}_2^{+}+\mathrm{NO}_3^{-} \\
& \mathrm{Na} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}^{+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \\
& \mathrm{NO}_2^{+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NO}_2 \uparrow \\
& \text { (brown) }
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

options: resulting mixture is neutralised by 1 mol of KOH

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: With reference to aqua regia, choose the correct option(s).
options: The yellow colour of aqua regia is due to the presence of NOCl and $\mathrm{Cl}_2$
Aqua regia is prepared by mixing conc. HCl and conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ in $3: 1$ (v/v) ratio
Reaction of gold with aqua regia produces an anion having Au in +3 oxidation state
Reaction of gold with aqua regia produces $\mathrm{NO}_2$ in the absence of air
questions.solution:
Answer (A, B, C)
(A) $\mathrm{HNO}_3+3 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NOCl}+\mathrm{Cl}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

The nitrosyl chloride and chlorine impart yellow color to aqua regia.
(B) Aqua regia is a $3: 1$ mixture of conc. HCl and conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$.
(C) Gold dissolves in aqua regia to form $\left[\mathrm{AuCl}_4\right]^{-}$.
$\mathrm{Au}+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{NO}_3^{-}+4 \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{AuCl}_4\right]^{-}+\mathrm{NO}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
(D) Air oxidises NO to $\mathrm{NO}_2$.

Hence in absence of air, possibility of formation of $\mathrm{NO}_2$ is less.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Which of the following statements are true?
options: Ionisation enthalpy of molecular oxygen is very close to that of xenon.
Hydrolysis of $\mathrm{XeF}_6$ may involve a redox reaction.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{ClO}_2$ is powerful oxidising agent, also strong chlorinating agent. Its bleaching power is almost 30 times stronger than $\mathrm{Cl}_2$. In alkaline solution undergoes disproportionation.
$2 \mathrm{ClO}_2+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaClO}+\mathrm{NaClO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The correct statement(s) about the oxoacids, $\mathrm{HClO}_4$ and HClO , is(are)
options: The conjugate base of $\mathrm{HClO}_4$ is weaker base than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
The central atom in both $\mathrm{HClO}_4$ and HClO is $\mathrm{sp}^3$ hybridized
$\mathrm{HClO}_4$ is formed in the reaction between $\mathrm{Cl}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
$\mathrm{HClO}_4$ is more acidic than HClO because of the resonance stabilization of its anion
questions.solution:
Answer (A, B, D) (A) $\mathrm{HClO}_4\gt\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \quad$ Acidic character $\mathrm{ClO}_4^{-} \lt \mathrm{OH}^{-} \quad$ Conjugate Base Strong acids have weak conjugate base (B) Both $\mathrm{HClO}_4$ and HClO central atom is $s p^3$ hybridised (D) $\mathrm{HClO}_4\gt\mathrm{HClO}$ Acidic strength $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{HClO}_4 \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{ClO}_4^{-} \\ & \mathrm{HClO} \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}^{+}+\mathrm{ClO}^{-} \end{aligned}$
In $\mathrm{ClO}_4^{-}$, nagative charge is dispersed on four oxygen, so better resonance stabilised.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Based on the compounds of group 15 elements, the correct statement(s) is (are)
options: The N-N single bond is stronger than the $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{P}$ single bond
questions.solution:
Answer (A, B, C)
Basic character of oxide decreases as we move down the group.
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{Bi}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$ is more basic than $\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$
- Covalent nature depends on the electronegativity difference between bonded atoms.
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{NF}_3$ is more covalent than $\mathrm{BiF}_3$
- Boiling point of $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is more (due to H-bonding) than $\mathrm{PH}_3$
- P-P single bond is stronger than $N-N$ single bond (as in $N$, due to smaller size of atoms lone pair-lone pair repulsion will be more)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution: Basic strength of nitrogen trihalides is inversely proportional to electronegativity of halogen. Stability of nitrogen trihalides is directly proportional to electronegativity difference between N -atom and halogen atom. Ease of hydrolysis of trichlorides of group-15 decreases down the group due to increase in metallic character of group-15 elements. Acidic strength of oxides of group-15 decreases down the group due to decrease in electronegativity.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
